title: Sylvara — Architecture and Technical Decisions
content:
# Sylvara — Architecture & Technical Decisions
> Created: 2026-03-13

## Infrastructure

### Railway
- Two services: `web` (Next.js) and `api` (Fastify) [EXPLICIT]
- PostgreSQL database hosted on Railway [EXPLICIT]
- Railway custom domain uses internal hostname (e.g., ttdhturu.up.railway.app), not the public URL [EXPLICIT]
- One-click Cloudflare Connect handles DNS→Railway routing automatically [EXPLICIT]
- Deploy: push to main → Railway auto-deploys both services [EXPLICIT]

### Cloudflare
- Domain `sylvara.app` registered on Cloudflare [EXPLICIT]
- DNS records: 5 Clerk CNAMEs + 1 Railway CNAME for scheduler.sylvara.app [EXPLICIT]
- SSL handled by Cloudflare proxy [INFERRED]

### PostgreSQL
- Hosted on Railway (not localhost in production) [EXPLICIT]
- All tables use UUID primary keys [EXPLICIT]
- Soft deletes (`deleted_at`) on every table [EXPLICIT]
- Prisma ORM with migrations (never edit committed migrations) [EXPLICIT]
- `numeric` types for money/hours — never use JS `number` for arithmetic [EXPLICIT]

## Auth Architecture

### Clerk Production
- Separate production instance cloned from dev [EXPLICIT]
- Production keys: `pk_live_*` / `sk_live_*` (vs `pk_test_*` / `sk_test_*` in dev) [EXPLICIT]
- User IDs are instance-specific — production IDs differ from dev [INFERRED]
- Domain restriction configured in Clerk dashboard [EXPLICIT]
- Clerk Branding (logo upload) requires paid tier — not available on free plan [EXPLICIT]

### Auth Flow
1. User signs in via Google OAuth on Clerk-hosted pages [EXPLICIT]
2. `user.created` webhook fires → Next.js webhook handler → upserts DB user + sets publicMetadata [EXPLICIT]
3. On authenticated requests: ClerkProvider (client) → clerkMiddleware (Next.js) → auth() in proxy route → getToken() → Bearer token forwarded to Fastify [EXPLICIT]
4. Fastify global preHandler: verifyToken from @clerk/backend → reads publicMetadata (userId, role) → sets request.actor [EXPLICIT]
5. Fallback: if publicMetadata missing, resolves user by clerkId from DB + fire-and-forget repair of stale metadata [EXPLICIT]

### Webhook Flow
- Clerk → POST /api/webhooks/clerk (on both Next.js and Fastify sides) [EXPLICIT]
- Svix signature verification before any processing [EXPLICIT]
- Fastify handler has a known bug: re-serializes parsed body instead of using raw bytes [EXPLICIT]
- Next.js handler correctly uses `request.text()` for raw body [EXPLICIT]
- Webhook is idempotent: findUnique by email, upsert pattern [EXPLICIT]

### Roles
- Three roles: MANAGER, SCHEDULER, VIEWER [EXPLICIT]
- New users default to VIEWER [EXPLICIT]
- MANAGER can promote/demote via Admin page [EXPLICIT]
- MANAGER/SCHEDULER can mutate; VIEWER is read-only [EXPLICIT]
- All authenticated users can read all data (by design for small team) [EXPLICIT]

## API / Proxy Architecture

### Next.js Proxy Pattern
- Frontend calls `/api/proxy?path=/api/...` [EXPLICIT]
- Proxy route: validates Clerk session → gets JWT token → forwards to Fastify API with Bearer header [EXPLICIT]
- Why: Next.js handles Clerk session cookies natively; Fastify only needs to verify the JWT [INFERRED]
- In production: `API_URL` env var points to Railway internal networking URL [EXPLICIT]
- In dev: defaults to `http://127.0.0.1:4000` [EXPLICIT]

### Fastify API
- Global auth preHandler on all `/api/*` routes (except `/api/health` and `/api/webhooks/*`) [EXPLICIT]
- Error responses: consistent shape `{ error: { code, message, details? } }` [EXPLICIT]
- Global error handler sanitizes 5xx responses — no stack traces leak [EXPLICIT]
- CORS configured via `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env var (must include `https://` prefix) [EXPLICIT]

## Key ADRs (2026-03-13)

### ADR: DNS — scheduler.sylvara.app
- **Decision:** App lives at `scheduler.sylvara.app`; root `sylvara.app` reserved for future marketing site [EXPLICIT]
- **Context:** Needed a clean production URL. Root domain may host a marketing/landing page later.

### ADR: Clerk — Production Instance Cloned from Dev
- **Decision:** Clone dev instance to production rather than creating fresh [EXPLICIT]
- **Context:** Preserves Google OAuth configuration and social login setup. Saves reconfiguration time.

### ADR: Google OAuth — New Production Credentials
- **Decision:** Create new Google OAuth credentials for production (Option B), not reuse dev client [EXPLICIT]
- **Context:** Dev credentials were tied to localhost redirect URIs. Cleaner to create dedicated production credentials with scheduler.sylvara.app redirect URIs.

### ADR: In-App Role Management
- **Decision:** Admin page replaces CLI `promote-manager` script [EXPLICIT]
- **Context:** Non-technical users (John, Eric) need to manage roles. CLI script was dev-only tooling.
- **Implementation:** PATCH `/api/admin/users/:id/role` and `/active` endpoints, gated by requireManagerPermission. Self-modification prevented.

### ADR: Self-Healing publicMetadata
- **Decision:** JWT auth fallback resolver repairs stale Clerk publicMetadata via fire-and-forget API call [EXPLICIT]
- **Context:** When webhook hasn't run or metadata is stale (e.g., after role change), the fallback resolver in jwt-auth.ts finds the user by clerkId in DB, returns correct role, and asynchronously updates Clerk metadata so future JWTs are correct.
- **File:** `apps/api/src/server.ts` — resolveByClerkId function


---
**2026-03-16T00:37:22.194316-07:00 | Created via Engram**
